Ezek.44: The east gate shut after the Prince enters; reformed Levitical service; only Zadokites approach.
Ezek.45: The holy district set apart; the Prince's portion; just weights; appointed feasts.
Ezek.46: Worship regulations — Sabbath and new moon offerings; the Prince's gate; kitchens for sacrifices.
Ezek.47: River from under the temple deepens — wherever it flows, life; tribal land boundaries reset.
Ezek.48: Tribal allotments around the holy portion; twelve gates; the city's name: The LORD Is There.
Dan.1: Daniel and friends resolve not to defile themselves with the king's food; God grants knowledge and skill.
Dan.2: Nebuchadnezzar's statue dream — four kingdoms broken by a stone cut without hands becoming a great mountain.
Dan.3: Fiery furnace — though he does not deliver, we will not bow; a fourth like a son of the gods walks with them.
Dan.4: Nebuchadnezzar's tree dream — humbled to eat grass; restored when he lifts his eyes to heaven.
Dan.5: Belshazzar's feast — handwriting on the wall; weighed and found wanting; Babylon falls that night.
Dan.6: Daniel in the lions' den — no harm because he trusted in his God; Darius decrees fear of the living God.
Dan.7: Four beasts and the Ancient of Days — the Son of Man receives an eternal kingdom; foundational to Christ's self-identification.
Dan.8: Ram and goat — Medo-Persia and Greece; the little horn (Antiochus) profanes the sanctuary 2,300 evenings and mornings.
Dan.9: Daniel prays over Jeremiah's seventy years; Gabriel reveals seventy weeks decreed — Messiah cut off but not for himself.
Dan.10: Vision by the Tigris of a glorious man; the prince of Persia withstands; Michael helps; do not fear, greatly loved.
Dan.11: Detailed prophecy of Persian and Greek kings; the king who exalts himself above every god foreshadows the antichrist.
Dan.12: Many sleeping in the dust shall awake — some to everlasting life, some to shame; seal the book till the end.
Hos.1: Hosea marries Gomer — God's covenant with faithless Israel acted out in a prophet's marriage.
Hos.2: I will allure her into the wilderness and speak tenderly; you will call me 'My Husband,' not 'My Baal.'
Hos.3: Buy her back — Hosea redeems Gomer for fifteen shekels of silver; Israel will return and seek David their king.
Hos.4: No faithfulness, no steadfast love, no knowledge of God in the land; my people are destroyed for lack of knowledge.
Hos.5: Priests and king ensnared; Ephraim sick; the LORD withdraws till they acknowledge their guilt and seek his face.
Hos.6: Come, let us return — he has torn that he may heal; I desire steadfast love, not sacrifice.
Hos.7: Ephraim a half-baked cake; a silly dove with no sense; they do not return to the Most High.
Hos.8: They sow the wind and reap the whirlwind; the calf of Samaria shall be broken to pieces.
Hos.9: No more rejoicing, O Israel; days of punishment have come; the prophet is a fool, the man of the spirit mad.
Hos.10: Israel a luxuriant vine — sow righteousness, reap steadfast love; break up your fallow ground; seek the LORD.
Hos.11: When Israel was a child I loved him, out of Egypt I called my son; how can I give you up, Ephraim?
Hos.12: Jacob striving with God at Peniel; return to your God, hold fast to love and justice.
Hos.13: I am the LORD your God from the land of Egypt; O Death, where are your plagues? — quoted by Paul of resurrection.
Hos.14: Return, O Israel — take with you words; I will heal their apostasy and love them freely.
Joel.1: Locust plague — call a solemn assembly; the day of the LORD is near, like destruction from the Almighty.
Joel.2: Mighty army of locusts/invaders; rend your hearts and not your garments; I will pour out my Spirit on all flesh.
Joel.3: Valley of Jehoshaphat — God judges the nations; multitudes in the valley of decision; the LORD a refuge for his people.
Amos.1: The lion roars from Zion — oracles against Damascus, Gaza, Tyre, Edom, Ammon for transgression upon transgression.
Amos.2: Judgment on Moab, Judah, and especially Israel — they sell the righteous for silver and trample the poor.
Amos.3: You only have I known of all families — therefore I will punish; can two walk together unless they agree?
Amos.4: Cows of Bashan rebuked; yet you did not return to me, says the LORD; prepare to meet your God.
Amos.5: Seek me and live; let justice roll down like waters; the day of the LORD is darkness, not light.
Amos.6: Woe to those at ease in Zion — first to anoint, last to grieve at Joseph's ruin.
Amos.7: Visions of locusts, fire, plumb line; Amaziah opposes Amos — I was no prophet, but a herdsman.
Amos.8: Basket of summer fruit — the end is come; famine of hearing the words of the LORD.
Amos.9: The LORD by the altar; sift Israel among the nations; raise up the fallen booth of David — restoration promise.
Obad.1: Edom's pride humbled — thou dweller in the clefts of the rock; saviors come to Mount Zion to judge Esau; the kingdom is the LORD's.
Jonah.1: Jonah flees to Tarshish; great storm; cast into the sea and swallowed by a great fish.
Jonah.2: From the fish's belly Jonah prays — salvation belongs to the LORD; the fish vomits him onto dry land.
Jonah.3: Nineveh repents at Jonah's eight-word sermon — the largest revival in the OT, carried out among Israel's enemies.
Jonah.4: Jonah angry under the withered plant; should I not pity Nineveh, where 120,000 cannot tell right from left?
Mic.1: The LORD comes down to tread the high places; Samaria's wound has reached Judah's gate.
Mic.2: Woe to schemers of injustice on their beds; God will gather a remnant like sheep in a fold.
Mic.3: Rulers who hate good and love evil; prophets who divine for money; Zion shall be plowed as a field.
Mic.4: Mountain of the LORD's house exalted; nations stream up; swords into plowshares; everyone under his vine.
Mic.5: Out of Bethlehem Ephrathah comes the ruler whose origins are from of old — the Shepherd of Israel's peace.
Mic.6: What does the LORD require? — to do justice, to love kindness, to walk humbly with your God.
Mic.7: Who is a God like you, pardoning iniquity? He will tread our sins underfoot and cast them into the sea.
Nah.1: The LORD is jealous and avenging — slow to anger, great in power; a stronghold to those who take refuge.
Nah.2: Nineveh's sack vividly portrayed — chariots rage, lions' den emptied; she is laid bare and carried off.
Nah.3: Woe to the bloody city — there is no easing your hurt; all who hear of you clap their hands.
Hab.1: Habakkuk asks why God tolerates evil; God's answer: I am raising the Chaldeans — more troubling still.
Hab.2: Stand on the watch; the righteous shall live by faith (cited by Paul); five woes; the earth filled with the knowledge of his glory.
Hab.3: Habakkuk's prayer-psalm: though the fig tree does not blossom, I will rejoice in the LORD — faith despite calamity.
Zeph.1: The great day of the LORD — sweep away everything; punish those who say the LORD will not do good or ill.
Zeph.2: Seek the LORD, all you humble of the land; oracles against Philistia, Moab, Ammon, Cush, Assyria.
Zeph.3: Yet the LORD in your midst is mighty to save — he will rejoice over you with singing, quiet you with his love.
Hag.1: Consider your ways — you build paneled houses while God's house lies in ruins; the people rouse and rebuild.
Hag.2: The latter glory of this house shall be greater; I will shake the nations; Zerubbabel my signet — Davidic line preserved.
Zech.1: Return to me and I will return to you; vision of horsemen patrolling earth; four horns and four craftsmen.
Zech.2: Man with measuring line — Jerusalem will be inhabited as villages; I will be a wall of fire around her.
Zech.3: Joshua the high priest in filthy garments; Satan accuses, the LORD rebukes; clean turban and the Branch promised.
Zech.4: Lampstand and two olive trees — not by might nor by power, but by my Spirit, says the LORD of hosts.
Zech.5: Flying scroll over the land; woman in the basket — wickedness carried to Shinar.
Zech.6: Four chariots from between bronze mountains; crown set on Joshua — the Branch will be both priest and king.
Zech.7: Concerning fasting — was it really for me you fasted? Render true judgments, show kindness and mercy.
Zech.8: I will return to Zion; old men and women in the streets; let your hands be strong; many peoples seek the LORD.
Zech.9: Behold, your King comes to you, righteous and having salvation, humble and mounted on a donkey — Palm Sunday foretold.
Zech.10: The LORD of hosts cares for his flock; out of Judah the cornerstone, the tent peg, the battle bow.
Zech.11: Two staves Favor and Union broken; the worthless shepherd; thirty pieces of silver thrown to the potter.
Zech.12: Jerusalem a cup of staggering; they will look on me, on him whom they have pierced, and mourn.
Zech.13: A fountain opened for sin and uncleanness; strike the shepherd, the sheep scatter; refined remnant.
Zech.14: The Day when the LORD's feet stand on the Mount of Olives; living waters from Jerusalem; the LORD shall be king over all the earth.
Mal.1: Jacob have I loved, Esau hated; you offer blind and lame sacrifices — would your governor accept them?
Mal.2: Priests rebuked for corrupt teaching; God hates divorce; weariness of the LORD with treachery in marriage.
Mal.3: Behold I send my messenger — refiner's fire; bring the full tithes; a book of remembrance for those who fear the LORD.
Mal.4: The Sun of Righteousness rises with healing in his wings; Elijah will come before the great day of the LORD.
Matt.1: Genealogy traces Messiah from Abraham through David to Joseph; virgin birth fulfills Isaiah's Immanuel — God with us.
Matt.2: Magi worship the infant King; Herod's slaughter of innocents; flight to Egypt fulfills 'out of Egypt I called my Son.'
Matt.3: John the Baptist's wilderness preaching of repentance; Jesus' baptism inaugurates ministry with Father's voice and Spirit's descent.
Matt.4: Wilderness temptation — Christ as second Adam resists Satan by Scripture; Galilean ministry begins; first disciples called.
Matt.5: Sermon on the Mount opens with the Beatitudes and Jesus' authoritative reading of the law — kingdom righteousness exceeds the Pharisees'.
Matt.6: Sermon continues — true piety in alms, prayer (Lord's Prayer), and fasting done before God, not men; treasure in heaven.
Matt.7: Sermon's close — judging, asking, golden rule, narrow gate, false prophets, two foundations: hearing and doing Christ's words.
Matt.8: Authority displayed — leper cleansed, centurion's faith, storm stilled, demons cast out; Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head.
Matt.9: Paralytic forgiven and healed; Matthew called; new wine in new wineskins; harvest is plentiful but laborers are few.
Matt.10: The Twelve commissioned — sheep among wolves, fear God not man, take up the cross; not peace but a sword.
Matt.11: John's question and Jesus' answer; woes on unrepentant cities; the Father revealed to babes; come to Me, all who labor.
Matt.12: Sabbath controversies; the unforgivable blasphemy against the Spirit; sign of Jonah; true family does the Father's will.
Matt.13: Kingdom parables — sower, weeds, mustard seed, leaven, treasure, pearl, net; mysteries hidden from the wise revealed to disciples.
Matt.14: John the Baptist beheaded; feeding of the five thousand; Jesus walks on water; Peter's faltering faith and worship.
Matt.15: Tradition vs commandment; defilement comes from the heart; Canaanite woman's great faith; feeding of the four thousand.
Matt.16: Peter's confession at Caesarea Philippi — 'You are the Christ'; first passion prediction; take up your cross.
Matt.17: Transfiguration glimpses kingdom glory; Moses and Elijah testify; demoniac boy healed; temple tax paid by miracle.
Matt.18: Greatness in the kingdom — humility of a child, care for little ones, church discipline, unlimited forgiveness in parable of unforgiving servant.
Matt.19: Marriage and divorce; little children blessed; rich young ruler walks away; first will be last in the kingdom.
Matt.20: Laborers in the vineyard — sovereign generosity; third passion prediction; James and John's request; servant greatness; blind men see.
Matt.21: Triumphal entry; cleansing of the temple; cursed fig tree; authority questioned; parables of two sons and wicked tenants.
Matt.22: Wedding feast parable; tribute to Caesar; resurrection answered; greatest commandment; David's Lord silences the Pharisees.
Matt.23: Seven woes on scribes and Pharisees — hypocrisy, blind guides, whitewashed tombs; lament over Jerusalem.
Matt.24: Olivet Discourse — temple's fall, signs of the end, abomination of desolation, coming of the Son of Man, watchfulness.
Matt.25: Ten virgins, talents, sheep and goats — readiness, faithful stewardship, and final judgment based on deeds toward Christ's brethren.
Matt.26: Anointing at Bethany; Last Supper instituted; Gethsemane agony; betrayal, arrest, Sanhedrin trial; Peter's denial.
Matt.27: Judas's remorse; Pilate's trial; Barabbas released; crucifixion, mockery, darkness, death; tomb sealed and guarded.
Matt.28: Resurrection morning — empty tomb, angelic announcement, risen Lord met; Great Commission to make disciples of all nations.
Mark.1: Forerunner, baptism, wilderness, calling of fishermen, demonic exorcisms — Mark's rapid-fire opening establishes Jesus' authority.
Mark.2: Paralytic forgiven and healed; Levi called; new wine, new wineskins; Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.
Mark.3: Withered hand on Sabbath; the Twelve appointed; blasphemy against the Spirit; Christ's true family.
Mark.4: Parable of the sower and the lamp; mustard seed; storm stilled — 'who then is this, that even wind and sea obey Him?'
Mark.5: Gerasene demoniac freed; Jairus's daughter raised; bleeding woman healed — Jesus reigns over demons, disease, and death.
Mark.6: Rejection at Nazareth; Twelve sent out; John beheaded; five thousand fed; Jesus walks on the sea.
Mark.7: Tradition versus commandment; defilement from within; Syrophoenician woman's faith; deaf-mute healed — He has done all things well.
Mark.8: Four thousand fed; Pharisees seek a sign; blind man healed in stages; Peter's confession; first passion prediction; take up the cross.
Mark.9: Transfiguration; demoniac boy healed; second passion prediction; greatness as servanthood; severe warnings against causing stumbling.
Mark.10: Marriage; children blessed; rich man departs sad; third passion prediction; James and John; blind Bartimaeus sees and follows.
Mark.11: Triumphal entry; fig tree cursed; temple cleansed; faith that moves mountains; Jesus' authority challenged.
Mark.12: Wicked tenants; tribute to Caesar; resurrection; greatest commandment; David's Lord; widow's two mites contrast scribal pride.
Mark.13: Olivet Discourse — temple's destruction, tribulation, Son of Man's coming; no one knows the day; watch and stay awake.
Mark.14: Anointing at Bethany; Passover prepared; Last Supper; Gethsemane; betrayal; trial before Caiaphas; Peter's denial.
Mark.15: Pilate's trial; Barabbas; mockery and crucifixion; centurion's confession — 'truly this man was the Son of God'; burial.
Mark.16: Resurrection morning at the empty tomb; angel's announcement; appearances; longer ending's commission and ascension.
Luke.1: Gabriel's announcements to Zechariah and Mary; Magnificat and Benedictus celebrate God's covenant mercy fulfilled in Christ and forerunner.
Luke.2: Bethlehem nativity; angelic glory to shepherds; Simeon and Anna recognize the Consolation of Israel; boy Jesus in His Father's house.
Luke.3: John's wilderness ministry of repentance; Jesus' baptism with Spirit and voice; genealogy traces Adam — Son of Man for all humanity.
Luke.4: Wilderness temptation overcome by Scripture; Nazareth sermon — 'today this Scripture is fulfilled'; Capernaum healings reveal kingdom power.
Luke.5: Miraculous catch and Peter's call; leper cleansed, paralytic forgiven; Levi's feast; new wine for new wineskins.
Luke.6: Sabbath controversies; Twelve chosen; Sermon on the Plain — beatitudes, woes, love of enemies, two builders.
Luke.7: Centurion's faith; widow of Nain's son raised; John's question answered; sinful woman anoints Jesus' feet — much forgiven, much love.
Luke.8: Parable of the sower; women supporters; storm stilled; Gerasene demoniac; Jairus's daughter and bleeding woman.
Luke.9: Twelve sent; five thousand fed; Peter's confession; Transfiguration; cost of discipleship — no looking back at the plow.
Luke.10: Seventy-two sent; woes on cities; Father reveals Son to babes; Good Samaritan defines neighbor; Mary chooses the good portion.
Luke.11: Lord's Prayer; persistence in prayer; Beelzebul charge refuted; sign of Jonah; woes on Pharisees and lawyers.
Luke.12: Beware hypocrisy; fear God; rich fool; do not be anxious; faithful stewards; Christ brings division before peace.
Luke.13: Repent or perish; bent woman healed on Sabbath; mustard seed and leaven; narrow door; lament over Jerusalem.
Luke.14: Sabbath healing; humility at table; great banquet; cost of discipleship — count the cost, bear the cross, be salt that retains savor.
Luke.15: Lost sheep, lost coin, prodigal son — heaven's joy over one repenting sinner; the Father runs to embrace the returning rebel.
Luke.16: Shrewd manager — use mammon for eternal friends; cannot serve two masters; rich man and Lazarus reveal eternal reversal.
Luke.17: Forbid offenses, forgive freely, increase faith; ten lepers — only Samaritan returns; the kingdom is in your midst; Son of Man's day.
Luke.18: Persistent widow; Pharisee and tax collector — the humbled is justified; children welcomed; rich ruler refuses; blind man sees.
Luke.19: Zacchaeus saved — Son of Man came to seek and save the lost; parable of the minas; triumphal entry and weeping over Jerusalem.
Luke.20: Authority questioned; wicked tenants; tribute to Caesar; resurrection answered; David's Lord; beware the scribes.
Luke.21: Widow's mites; Olivet Discourse — temple destruction, tribulation, redemption draws near; watch and pray always.
Luke.22: Last Supper instituted; greatness as service; Gethsemane agony with bloody sweat; betrayal, arrest; Peter's denial; trial before council.
Luke.23: Pilate and Herod find no fault; Barabbas released; via Dolorosa; crucifixion; penitent thief promised paradise; burial.
Luke.24: Resurrection morn; Emmaus road — opened Scriptures, opened eyes; risen Christ commissions; ascension from Bethany.
John.1: Eternal Word made flesh — Logos creates, enlightens, dwells among us; John bears witness; first disciples gathered; Lamb of God revealed.
John.2: Cana's water-to-wine reveals glory; temple cleansed; Jesus knows what is in man — sign and authority of the new covenant.
John.3: Nicodemus and new birth — flesh begets flesh, Spirit begets Spirit. John 3:16 anchors the gospel: belief in the lifted-up Son brings eternal life.
John.4: Samaritan woman at the well — living water for thirsty souls; true worshipers worship in spirit and truth; nobleman's son healed.
John.5: Bethesda paralytic healed on Sabbath; Son does what Father does; fivefold witness — Father, works, John, Scriptures, Moses.
John.6: Five thousand fed; walking on water; Bread of Life discourse — many disciples turn back; Peter: 'to whom shall we go?'
John.7: Tabernacles tension; rivers of living water; division over Christ; officers return empty-handed — 'no man ever spoke like this Man.'
John.8: Woman caught in adultery; Light of the World; truth sets free; before Abraham was, I AM — they take up stones.
John.9: Man born blind healed — sight given physically and spiritually; Pharisees blind in their seeing; the Shepherd seeks the cast-out.
John.10: Good Shepherd lays down His life for the sheep; sheep hear His voice; one flock, one Shepherd; the Father and I are one.
John.11: Lazarus raised — 'I am the resurrection and the life'; Jesus weeps; Caiaphas unwittingly prophesies substitutionary death.
John.12: Mary anoints Jesus; triumphal entry; Greeks seek Him; grain of wheat must die; lifted up, He draws all to Himself.
John.13: Upper Room — Jesus washes feet, modeling servant love; Judas dismissed; new commandment to love as He has loved.
John.14: 'I am the way, the truth, and the life' — Father's house, promised Helper; peace not as the world gives.
John.15: True Vine and branches — abide in Me to bear fruit; greater love has no man; world will hate as it hated Him.
John.16: Spirit will convict, guide into truth, glorify Christ; sorrow turned to joy; in the world tribulation, but He has overcome.
John.17: High Priestly Prayer — for Himself, His disciples, and all who will believe; that they may be one as Father and Son are one.
John.18: Gethsemane arrest — 'I AM' fells the cohort; Annas and Caiaphas; Peter's denials; Pilate asks 'what is truth?'
John.19: Scourging, mockery, crucifixion; mother committed to John; 'It is finished'; pierced side fulfills Scripture; burial by Joseph and Nicodemus.
John.20: Empty tomb; Mary Magdalene meets the risen Lord; Spirit-breathed commission; Thomas confesses 'My Lord and my God!'
John.21: Sea of Galilee — miraculous catch; Peter restored thrice; 'feed my sheep'; the disciple whom Jesus loved and the gospel's witness.
Acts.1: Forty days of resurrection appearances; ascension from Olivet; promise of Spirit and return; Matthias chosen to replace Judas.
Acts.2: Pentecost — Spirit poured out, tongues, Peter's sermon proclaiming risen Christ; three thousand baptized; community life established.
Acts.3: Lame man healed at Beautiful Gate; Peter preaches Christ as the Prophet like Moses; times of refreshing in repentance.
Acts.4: Sanhedrin's first persecution; salvation in no other name; church prays for boldness; all things held in common.
Acts.5: Ananias and Sapphira's deceit judged; apostles imprisoned, freed, beaten; Gamaliel's counsel; rejoicing to suffer for the Name.
Acts.6: Seven deacons appointed to serve tables; Stephen's wisdom and signs; false witnesses arise against him.
Acts.7: Stephen's sweeping survey of redemptive history indicts Israel's resistance; first martyr sees the Son of Man at God's right hand.
Acts.8: Persecution scatters the church; gospel to Samaria through Philip; Simon Magus rebuked; Ethiopian eunuch hears Isaiah 53 and believes.
Acts.9: Saul on the Damascus road meets the risen Christ; Ananias baptizes him; Aeneas healed; Dorcas raised by Peter.
Acts.10: Cornelius's vision; Peter's rooftop sheet — God shows no partiality; Spirit falls on Gentiles; baptism affirms inclusion.
Acts.11: Peter defends his actions to Jerusalem; Antioch becomes a sending church; disciples first called Christians; famine relief sent.
Acts.12: Herod kills James, imprisons Peter; angel delivers Peter; Herod struck down for accepting divine honor; word continues to grow.
Acts.13: Antioch sends Barnabas and Saul; first missionary journey; Paul's Pisidian Antioch sermon; gospel turned to Gentiles.
Acts.14: Iconium, Lystra, Derbe — signs and persecution; Paul stoned and revived; elders appointed; first journey concluded.
Acts.15: Jerusalem Council — salvation by grace through faith apart from circumcision; James's decree; Paul and Barnabas part ways.
Acts.16: Timothy joins; Macedonian call; Lydia's heart opened; Philippian jailer's household saved through earthquake and gospel.
Acts.17: Thessalonica and Berea — searching the Scriptures daily; Athens — Areopagus address proclaims the unknown God and the risen Judge.
Acts.18: Corinth — Aquila and Priscilla; eighteen-month ministry under divine encouragement; Apollos taught the way more accurately.
Acts.19: Ephesus — Spirit given, hall of Tyrannus, sons of Sceva, books burned; Demetrius incites the silversmiths' riot.
Acts.20: Eutychus raised; Miletus farewell to Ephesian elders — guard the flock against wolves; more blessed to give than receive.
Acts.21: Journey to Jerusalem despite warnings; Paul's purification at the temple; mob seizes him; Roman tribune intervenes.
Acts.22: Paul addresses the crowd in Hebrew, recounting his conversion and Gentile commission; Roman citizenship halts scourging.
Acts.23: Sanhedrin divided over resurrection; conspiracy to kill Paul exposed; transferred under guard to Felix in Caesarea.
Acts.24: Tertullus accuses; Paul's defense before Felix — righteousness, self-control, judgment make Felix tremble; two-year detention.
Acts.25: Festus succeeds Felix; Paul appeals to Caesar; Festus consults King Agrippa about the puzzling charges.
Acts.26: Paul before Agrippa — conversion retold; almost persuaded; nothing worthy of death — chains alone hold him.
Acts.27: Voyage to Rome; storm and shipwreck; angelic assurance; all 276 souls saved as Paul foretold.
Acts.28: Malta's viper and healings; arrival in Rome; Paul preaches the kingdom and teaches the Lord Jesus with all boldness, unhindered.
Rom.1: Gospel revealed; Gentile suppression of truth leads to God's giving them up; the righteous shall live by faith.
Rom.2: Jewish presumption rebuked; God judges impartially; true circumcision is of the heart by the Spirit, not the letter.
Rom.3: All under sin — none righteous, none seek God; righteousness through faith in Christ as propitiation; boasting excluded.
Rom.4: Abraham justified by faith before circumcision and law; David's blessing of imputed righteousness; faith credited apart from works.
Rom.5: Peace, access, hope; suffering produces character; Adam and Christ — through one Man's obedience the many are made righteous.
Rom.6: Dead to sin, alive to God in Christ; baptized into His death; reckon yourselves dead to sin; wages of sin vs gift of God.
Rom.7: Released from the law to be married to Christ; the law is holy; the wretched man cries for deliverance from this body of death.
Rom.8: From no-condemnation to no-separation: life in the Spirit, adoption, present suffering vs future glory, the unbreakable golden chain.
Rom.9: God's sovereign election — vessels of mercy and wrath; Israel's stumbling not God's failure; the potter has rights over the clay.
Rom.10: Christ is the end of the law for righteousness; confess and believe; faith comes by hearing the word of Christ.
Rom.11: Israel's hardening is partial and temporary; Gentile branches grafted in; all Israel will be saved; depths of God's wisdom.
Rom.12: Living sacrifices, transformed minds; one body with many gifts; love without hypocrisy; overcome evil with good.
Rom.13: Submission to governing authorities; love fulfills the law; the night is far gone — put on the Lord Jesus Christ.
Rom.14: Welcome the weak in faith; do not judge over disputable matters; the kingdom is righteousness, peace, joy in the Spirit.
Rom.15: Bear with the weak; Christ as servant of both Jew and Gentile; Paul's missionary ambition to preach where Christ is unnamed.
Rom.16: Personal greetings to many saints in Rome; warnings against divisive teachers; doxology to the only wise God through Jesus Christ.
1Cor.1: Christ crucified — folly to Gentiles, scandal to Jews, but God's wisdom and power; God chose the foolish to shame the wise.
1Cor.2: Paul preached Christ crucified in weakness; spiritual things spiritually discerned; we have the mind of Christ.
1Cor.3: Carnal Corinthians factional over leaders; ministers are servants; foundation is Christ; build with gold or wood, fire will test.
1Cor.4: Stewards required to be faithful; apostles a spectacle to the world; Paul as father in the gospel through Christ Jesus.
1Cor.5: Incest in the church requires removal — purge the old leaven; deliver him to Satan for restoration; Christ our Passover sacrificed.
1Cor.6: Lawsuits among brethren shame the gospel; the unrighteous will not inherit the kingdom; flee fornication — your body is the Spirit's temple.
1Cor.7: Marriage and singleness; remain in the calling wherein one was called; the time is short — live as though not engrossed by this world.
1Cor.8: Food offered to idols; knowledge puffs up but love builds up; do not let your liberty wound a weak brother for whom Christ died.
1Cor.9: Apostolic rights surrendered for the gospel; become all things to all men; run to win; discipline body lest disqualified.
1Cor.10: Israel's wilderness as warning; flee idolatry; cup of blessing communion; do all to the glory of God.
1Cor.11: Headship and worship order; Lord's Supper — examine yourself, discern the body; many sick because of irreverence.
1Cor.12: Spiritual gifts diverse but one Spirit; the body has many members; God appoints; greater gifts to be desired.
1Cor.13: The love chapter — Paul's hymn defines agape between his treatments of spiritual gifts. Without love, gifts are nothing.
1Cor.14: Prophecy preferred over tongues for edifying the church; let all things be done decently and in order.
1Cor.15: Resurrection chapter — Christ raised, firstfruits; if no resurrection, faith is futile; death swallowed up in victory.
1Cor.16: Collection for the saints; travel plans; final exhortations — watch, stand fast in the faith, be strong; maranatha.
2Cor.1: God of all comfort comforts us in affliction so we may comfort others; sentence of death taught reliance on God who raises the dead.
2Cor.2: Forgive the repentant offender; triumphal procession in Christ; we are the aroma of life or death — sufficient for these things?
2Cor.3: Letter from Christ written by the Spirit on hearts; new covenant ministry exceeds Moses' fading glory; we behold and are transformed.
2Cor.4: Treasure in jars of clay; afflicted but not crushed; the inward man is renewed daily; light affliction, eternal weight of glory.
2Cor.5: Earthly tent and heavenly building; walk by faith not sight; ministry of reconciliation — God made Him sin for us.
2Cor.6: Now is the day of salvation; commended as ministers in every hardship; do not be unequally yoked with unbelievers.
2Cor.7: Cleansing pursued in fear of God; godly sorrow produces repentance unto life; Paul's joy at Corinthian repentance.
2Cor.8: Macedonian generosity in deep poverty; Christ became poor that we might become rich; equality through gracious giving.
2Cor.9: Sow bountifully, reap bountifully; God loves a cheerful giver; thanks to God for His indescribable gift.
2Cor.10: Spiritual warfare — weapons mighty to demolish strongholds; bringing every thought captive to obey Christ; let him who boasts boast in the Lord.
2Cor.11: Paul's fool's boasting — beware false apostles, Satan's ministers as angels of light; sufferings for Christ catalogued.
2Cor.12: Caught up to third heaven; thorn in flesh — My grace is sufficient, strength perfected in weakness; signs of an apostle.
2Cor.13: Examine yourselves whether you are in the faith; final warnings; benediction of grace, love, and fellowship of the triune God.
Gal.1: No other gospel — even an angel preaching another is anathema; Paul's gospel received by revelation, not from man.
Gal.2: Jerusalem affirms Paul's gospel; Peter rebuked at Antioch for hypocrisy; justified by faith in Christ, not works of the law.
Gal.3: Foolish Galatians — bewitched by works; Abraham believed and was reckoned righteous; the law was a tutor leading us to Christ.
Gal.4: Sons not slaves — heirs through the Spirit of adoption crying Abba; Hagar and Sarah allegory; Paul's anguish until Christ is formed in them.
Gal.5: Stand fast in liberty — Christ profits nothing if circumcised for righteousness; walk by the Spirit; fruit vs works of the flesh.
Gal.6: Bear one another's burdens; sow to the Spirit and reap eternal life; boast only in the cross of Christ — new creation matters.
Eph.1: Trinitarian blessing — Father chose, Son redeemed, Spirit sealed; predestination to adoption; prayer for enlightened hearts.
Eph.2: Dead in sins, made alive with Christ; saved by grace through faith — not of works; Jew and Gentile one new man, fellow citizens.
Eph.3: Mystery revealed — Gentiles fellow heirs; Paul's stewardship; prayer for inner strength to grasp Christ's love that surpasses knowledge.
Eph.4: One body, one Spirit, one Lord; gifted leaders equip saints; put off the old man, put on the new in true righteousness.
Eph.5: Walk in love, light, and wisdom; do not get drunk with wine but be filled with the Spirit; husbands love wives as Christ loved the church.
Eph.6: Children, parents, slaves, masters; put on the whole armor of God to stand against the schemes of the devil; pray in the Spirit always.
Phil.1: To live is Christ, to die is gain; partnership in the gospel; let your conduct be worthy of the gospel of Christ.
Phil.2: Christ's humiliation and exaltation — kenosis hymn; work out salvation with fear; shine as lights in a crooked generation.
Phil.3: All counted loss for the surpassing worth of knowing Christ; press toward the upward call; our citizenship is in heaven.
Phil.4: Rejoice always; do not be anxious — the peace of God guards your hearts; I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me.
Col.1: Supremacy of Christ — image of God, Creator, Head of the church, Reconciler; Paul's mystery: Christ in you, the hope of glory.
Col.2: All treasures of wisdom in Christ; beware philosophy and Jewish ritual; you are complete in Him who disarmed the powers at the cross.
Col.3: Set your mind on things above; put off the old, put on the new; let the word of Christ dwell richly; whatever you do, do for the Lord.
Col.4: Devotion to prayer; walk in wisdom toward outsiders; speech seasoned with salt; personal greetings and final instructions.
1Thess.1: Faith, love, and hope evident; the gospel came in power and the Spirit; turned from idols to serve the living God and await His Son.
1Thess.2: Paul's gentle apostolic conduct as a nursing mother and exhorting father; the Thessalonians are his glory and joy at Christ's coming.
1Thess.3: Timothy sent to strengthen them in affliction; great relief at their faith; prayer that the Lord would establish their hearts blameless.
1Thess.4: Sanctification in sexual purity and brotherly love; the Lord will descend with a shout — the dead in Christ rise first.
1Thess.5: Day of the Lord comes as a thief; sons of light, not darkness; rejoice always, pray without ceasing, give thanks in everything.
2Thess.1: Affliction repaid by God's righteous judgment when Christ is revealed in flaming fire with His mighty angels.
2Thess.2: Day of the Lord not yet — apostasy and man of lawlessness must come first; God's restraint and ultimate judgment.
2Thess.3: Pray for the spread of the word; warning against the disorderly — if any will not work, neither shall he eat; the Lord of peace be with you.
1Tim.1: Sound doctrine versus myths; the law is good if used lawfully; Paul as chief of sinners shows Christ's perfect patience.
1Tim.2: Pray for all men; one God, one Mediator who gave Himself a ransom; instructions on worship and the role of women.
1Tim.3: Qualifications for overseers and deacons; the church is the pillar and ground of the truth; mystery of godliness — God manifest in flesh.
1Tim.4: Last days apostasy foretold; bodily exercise vs godliness; let no one despise your youth; give attention to reading and doctrine.
1Tim.5: Honor widows truly in need; elders worthy of double honor; do not be hasty in laying on hands; keep yourself pure.
1Tim.6: Slaves and masters; godliness with contentment is great gain; flee the love of money; fight the good fight of faith.
2Tim.1: Stir up the gift of God; not a spirit of fear but of power, love, and a sound mind; guard the good deposit by the Spirit.
2Tim.2: Be strong in grace; soldier, athlete, farmer; the firm foundation of God stands; flee youthful lusts, be a vessel for honor.
2Tim.3: Last days perilous times; from a child you have known the Scriptures — all Scripture is God-breathed and profitable.
2Tim.4: Preach the word in season and out; I have fought the good fight, finished the course, kept the faith — a crown of righteousness awaits.
Titus.1: Qualifications for elders; rebuke false teachers in Crete sharply; to the pure all things are pure.
Titus.2: Sound doctrine for old and young, slaves and masters; the grace of God has appeared, training us while we await the blessed hope.
Titus.3: Be subject to rulers; saved by the washing of regeneration and renewal of the Spirit, not by works of righteousness which we have done.
Phlm.1: Paul intercedes for runaway Onesimus, now a beloved brother — receive him as you would me; the gospel transforms social relations.
Heb.1: God has spoken in His Son — heir, Creator, exact imprint, sustainer; superior to angels who are ministering spirits.
Heb.2: Pay closer attention lest we drift; Jesus made lower than angels to taste death for everyone; merciful and faithful High Priest.
Heb.3: Christ greater than Moses — Son over the house, not servant in it; harden not your hearts as in the wilderness rebellion.
Heb.4: Sabbath rest remains; the word is living and sharp, exposing all things; draw near boldly to the throne of grace through our High Priest.
Heb.5: Christ appointed High Priest after the order of Melchizedek; learned obedience through suffering; rebuke for spiritual immaturity.
Heb.6: Warning against falling away after enlightenment; God's promise to Abraham confirmed by oath — anchor of the soul, sure and steadfast.
Heb.7: Melchizedek's priesthood greater than Levi's; Christ's eternal priesthood guarantees a better covenant — He always lives to intercede.
Heb.8: Christ as High Priest of a better covenant established on better promises — God's law written on the heart, sins remembered no more.
Heb.9: Earthly tabernacle a copy; Christ entered heaven by His own blood, securing eternal redemption — appointed once to die, then judgment.
Heb.10: By one offering He has perfected the sanctified forever; draw near, hold fast, stir up; do not throw away your confidence.
Heb.11: The Hall of Faith — Abel through the prophets, all bearing witness that 'the just shall live by faith.' Foreshadows 12's 'great cloud of witnesses.'
Heb.12: Run with endurance looking to Jesus; God disciplines sons; do not refuse Him who speaks; Mount Zion, not Sinai — kingdom unshakable.
Heb.13: Brotherly love, hospitality, marriage, contentment; Jesus the same yesterday, today, forever; let us go to Him outside the camp.
Jas.1: Trials produce steadfastness; ask wisdom in faith; be doers, not hearers only; pure religion — care for orphans and widows, keep unstained.
Jas.2: No partiality — love your neighbor; faith without works is dead; Abraham and Rahab justified by faith working through deeds.
Jas.3: The tongue is a small fire that sets life ablaze; wisdom from above is pure, peaceable, gentle, full of mercy and good fruits.
Jas.4: Wars come from passions; submit to God, resist the devil; do not boast about tomorrow — to him who knows to do good and does it not, it is sin.
Jas.5: Woe to oppressive rich; be patient until the Lord's coming; prayer of the righteous avails much; restore the wandering sinner.
1Pet.1: Living hope through Christ's resurrection; faith tested as gold by fire; born again through the imperishable seed of God's word.
1Pet.2: Living stones in a spiritual house; royal priesthood, holy nation; Christ our example in suffering — by His stripes we are healed.
1Pet.3: Wives and husbands; suffering for righteousness' sake is blessed; Christ suffered once for sins, the just for the unjust, to bring us to God.
1Pet.4: Arm yourselves with the mind of Christ; love covers a multitude of sins; rejoice as you share Christ's sufferings; judgment begins at God's house.
1Pet.5: Elders shepherd willingly; humble yourselves under God's mighty hand; cast all anxiety on Him; resist the devil firm in faith.
2Pet.1: Divine power has granted all things for life and godliness; add to your faith virtue; we have the more sure prophetic word.
2Pet.2: False teachers like Balaam — judgment certain; God knows how to deliver the godly and reserve the unjust for judgment.
2Pet.3: Scoffers in the last days; the Lord is not slack — He delays in mercy; the day of the Lord will come; new heavens and new earth.
1John.1: The Word of life proclaimed; God is light; if we walk in the light, the blood of Christ cleanses us from all sin.
1John.2: Christ our Advocate and propitiation; love not the world; antichrists deny the Son; the anointing teaches you all things.
1John.3: Behold what manner of love — children of God; sin and righteousness mark the children; love one another as Christ commanded.
1John.4: Test the spirits; God is love — He first loved us; perfect love casts out fear; we love because He first loved us.
1John.5: Faith overcomes the world; the Spirit, water, and blood testify; he who has the Son has life; that you may know you have eternal life.
2John.1: Walk in truth and love; refuse hospitality to deceivers who deny that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh — share not in their evil works.
3John.1: Gaius commended for hospitality to traveling brethren; Diotrephes rebuked for love of preeminence; Demetrius commended.
Jude.1: Contend earnestly for the faith once delivered; warnings from Israel, angels, and Sodom; doxology — to Him who is able to keep you from falling.
Rev.1: Vision of the glorified Christ among the lampstands — Alpha and Omega, the First and the Last, who holds the keys of death and Hades.
Rev.2: Letters to Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira — commendation, rebuke, and call to overcome with promised reward.
Rev.3: Letters to Sardis, Philadelphia, Laodicea — wake up, hold fast, repent of lukewarmness; behold, I stand at the door and knock.
Rev.4: Throne room vision — God surrounded by the four living creatures and twenty-four elders crying 'Holy, holy, holy.'
Rev.5: The Lamb who was slain alone is worthy to open the scroll; new song of redemption from every tribe and tongue and nation.
Rev.6: Six seals opened — four horsemen, martyrs under the altar, cosmic upheaval; the great day of the Lamb's wrath has come.
Rev.7: 144,000 sealed from Israel; great multitude from every nation before the throne — they have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb.
Rev.8: Seventh seal brings silence; seven trumpets begin — first four sound judgments on earth, sea, rivers, and heavens.
Rev.9: Fifth and sixth trumpets — locusts from the abyss and demonic cavalry; mankind still does not repent of idolatry and immorality.
Rev.10: Mighty angel with the little scroll; seven thunders sealed; John eats the scroll — sweet in mouth, bitter in stomach.
Rev.11: Two witnesses prophesy, are killed, and resurrected; seventh trumpet — the kingdom of the world has become the kingdom of our Lord.
Rev.12: Woman, child, and dragon — Satan cast down, makes war with the offspring; they overcame by the blood of the Lamb and the word of testimony.
Rev.13: Beast from the sea and beast from the land; mark of the beast — call for the endurance and faith of the saints.
Rev.14: Lamb on Mount Zion with the 144,000; three angels' messages; harvest of the earth and winepress of God's wrath.
Rev.15: Seven angels with seven last plagues; song of Moses and the Lamb — great and marvelous are Your works, Lord God Almighty.
Rev.16: Seven bowls of wrath poured out — sores, blood, fire, darkness, Euphrates, Armageddon; 'It is done!' from the throne.
Rev.17: Mystery Babylon — the great harlot drunk with the blood of the saints; the Lamb will overcome, for He is Lord of lords.
Rev.18: Fall of Babylon the great — kings, merchants, and seafarers mourn; God has remembered her iniquities; come out of her, my people.
Rev.19: Hallelujahs in heaven; marriage supper of the Lamb; rider on the white horse — King of kings and Lord of lords — defeats the beast.
Rev.20: Satan bound a thousand years; great white throne judgment; death and Hades cast into the lake of fire; books opened.
Rev.21: The new heaven and new earth — God dwelling with men, every tear wiped, no more death. Eschatological hope made tangible.
Rev.22: River of life and tree of life; come, Lord Jesus; I am the Alpha and the Omega; the grace of the Lord Jesus be with all. Amen.
